candidates.target: SAXPY loop vectorization and memory throughput
candidates.rationale: Kernel is memory-bandwidth bound; ensuring aligned, non-aliasing pointers and enabling aggressive vectorization/unrolling can reduce loop overhead and improve sustained bandwidth while keeping the algorithm unchanged.
candidates.action_sketch: Add restrict qualifiers to x/y/z pointers and use compiler hints for alignment and vectorization on the SAXPY loop (e.g., assume_aligned/ivdep/simd pragmas); build with -O3 -march=native and optionally -ffast-math or -fno-math-errno depending on tolerance policy.
preconditions: Target compiler supports alignment and SIMD/vectorization directives (e.g., GCC/Clang/ICC) and build system can pass CPU-specific flags
x, y, z are 64-byte aligned and do not alias each other (true given separate aligned_alloc calls)
correctness_checks: Verify checksum remains within a defined numeric tolerance if enabling FP-reordering flags (e.g., abs(delta_checksum) <= 1 or relative error <= 1e-12 on s before scaling)
Run with multiple n and reps values to ensure no out-of-bounds or alignment-related crashes
rollback_criteria: If checksum deviates beyond tolerance or performance regresses by >5% compared to baseline -O3, revert directives/flags to baseline
